answers.field_label: Please Use This Area To Explain Your Cut Instructions In Detail
answers.value:
Customer vehicle is a 2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab, 6'4" box, short bed, 4x4, hit hard on the passenger side rear cab corner and upper B-pillar area. We are sourcing a recycled quad cab shell to section in the right rear cab corner, outer B-pillar skin, and a portion of the roof skin. We do NOT want a full cab; we only need the outer sheet metal and structure in the specified area.

GENERAL NOTES:
- Truck configuration: 2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab (four doors, smaller rear doors, NOT Crew Cab).
- We want a cut from a rust-free, non-salvage, non-rolled donor if possible. Please avoid units with prior structural repairs in the requested area.
- No floor pan or rocker inner is needed beyond what is specified; we primarily need outer skin and outer structure. Inner reinforcements can be left attached if they are part of a clean, straight cut.

DETAILED CUT LOCATION – PASSENGER SIDE (RIGHT SIDE):
1. B-PILLAR AREA:
   - Include the entire right-side B-pillar from the roof rail down to approximately 2 inches below the lower door striker area.
   - Cut through the rocker area: come down the B-pillar and include about 4 inches of the outer rocker panel forward and rearward of the B-pillar, but you do NOT need to include the full rocker length. A rectangular section around the B-pillar base, roughly 8–10 inches wide total, is fine.
   - At the roof, include the B-pillar up into the roof rail: cut across the roof approximately 4–5 inches inboard from the outer roof edge so we have enough material to work with for our section. Please keep the factory spot weld flange intact where possible.

2. REAR CAB CORNER – PASSENGER SIDE:
   - We need the outer rear cab corner from the body line just below the rear window down to approximately 3–4 inches below the lower edge of the rear door opening.
   - Forward/Rearward: Start the cut just aft of the B-pillar (include 3–4 inches of metal behind the B-pillar) and extend rearward all the way to the back seam of the cab corner, including the full radius of the corner where it wraps around toward the back of the cab.
   - Please include the entire outer corner skin, including the seam where it meets the back panel of the cab.

3. ROOF SECTION – PASSENGER SIDE FRONT TO REAR:
   - We need a roof section running above the right-side B-pillar and rear door opening.
   - Front-to-back: Start your cut just ahead of the B-pillar (about 3–4 inches forward of the B-pillar centerline) and continue rearward to just past the center of the rear cab corner window opening (or where the fixed rear glass would be). This gives us overlap to position our section.
   - Side-to-side: From the passenger-side drip rail/outer roof edge, come inboard about 4–5 inches and make a straight longitudinal cut. We do not need the full width of the roof, only the right outer strip.

4. REAR CAB PANEL (BACK OF CAB) – PASSENGER SIDE UPPER PORTION:
   - Please include the upper right portion of the back wall of the cab where it ties into the rear cab corner and roof.
   - Vertical extent: From the roof seam down to just below the midpoint of the rear glass opening (if the donor has a full rear window, cut through the opening about mid-height). We mainly want the sheet metal and structure that ties the rear cab corner into the back panel.
   - Horizontal extent: From the passenger side edge (where the rear corner wraps around) inboard about 8–10 inches.

5. WHAT TO EXCLUDE:
   - Do NOT include doors, glass, interior trim, or seats.
   - Do NOT include the rear sliding glass assembly; bare metal around the window is fine.
   - No dash, front clip, or frame rails are needed.
   - No floor pan beyond 2–3 inches of the inner rocker area directly under the B-pillar is necessary.

6. CUT QUALITY / HANDLING REQUESTS:
   - Please make straight, clean cuts where possible to give us good reference edges.
   - Leave factory seams, spot weld flanges, and pinch welds intact whenever you can, especially along the roof rail, door openings, and rear corner seams.
   - Do not torch-cut near the outer skin if it can be avoided; a saw cut is preferred to minimize heat distortion and cleanup.
   - Leave any attached inner reinforcements, brackets, or gussets that are welded inside the B-pillar or rear cab corner. We can drill or trim them as needed in the shop.

7. RUST / DAMAGE CONDITIONS:
   - We are in Iowa, so please avoid donors with heavy rust in the rocker or cab corner area. Light surface rust is acceptable, but no perforation or major scaling.
   - The B-pillar and roof rail must be straight and free of prior collision damage. Minor dings are acceptable as long as there is no structural compromise.

8. IDENTIFICATION / COMPATIBILITY:
   - Donor must be a 2013–2018 Ram 1500 Quad Cab (not Crew Cab) for sheet metal and body line compatibility.
   - Same body style and cab configuration is important so that the body lines and door openings line up with our 2018 truck.

9. SHIPPING / PACKAGING NOTES:
   - Please band the cut section to a pallet and protect sharp edges so it can be safely unloaded with a forklift.
   - Wrap exposed edges with cardboard or foam where practical to minimize shipping dents.

If any of the above is unclear, please call before cutting so we can adjust the cut lines. We would rather have a slightly larger section than too small; extra metal can be trimmed on our end.
